Effects of Mini-Batch size|D|: As mentioned in the Lemma 1, |D|is one of the parameters which can be controlled to adjust the value of Оҙ. In Fig. 3, when the size of the mini-batch is increased, pFedMe has the higher convergence rate. However, very large |D|will not only slow the convergence of pFedMe but also requires higher computations at the local users. During the experiments, the value of |D|is conп¬Ғgured as a constant value equal to 20. Effects of regularizationО»: Fig. 4 shows the convergence rate of pFedMe with different values of О». In all settings, larger О»allows for faster convergence; however, we also observe that the signiп¬Ғcantly large О»will hurt the performance of pFedMe by making pFedMe diverge. Therefore, О»should be tuned carefully depending on the dataset. We п¬Ғx О»= 15 for all scenarios with MNIST. Effects ofОІ: Fig. 5 illustrates how ОІ (ОІ вүҘ1) affects both the personalized and global models. It is noted that when ОІ = 1, it is similar to model averaging of FedAvg. According to the п¬Ғgure, it is beneп¬Ғcial to shift the value of ОІ to be larger as it allows pFedMe to converge faster, especially the global model. However, turning ОІcarefully is also signiп¬Ғcant to prevent the divergence and instability of pFedMe. For example, when ОІmoves to the large value, to stabilize the global model as well as the personalized model, the smaller value of О·needs to be considered. Alternatively, ОІand О·should be adjusted in inverse proportion to reach the stability of pFedMe. 5.3 Performance Comparison In order to highlight the empirical performance of pFedMe, we perform several comparisons between pFedMe, FedAvg, and Per-FedAvg. We п¬Ғrst use the same parameters for all algorithms as an initial comparison. Table. 1 shows that when using п¬Ғne-tuned hyperparameters, the pFedMeвҖҷs personalized model is the best performer in all settings. For Synthetic dataset, the comparisons for the utilizing the same parameters and the п¬Ғne-tuned parameter are presented in Fig. 7 and Table. 1, respectively. In Fig. 7, even though the global model of pFedMe is less well-performed than others concerning testing accuracy and training loss,pFedMeвҖҷs personalized model still shows its advantages as achieving the highest testing accuracy and smallest training loss. Fig. 7 shows that pFedMeвҖҷs personalized model is 6.1%, 3.8%, and 5.2% more accurate than its global model, Per-FedAvg, and FedAvg, respectively. The corresponding п¬Ғgures for the nonconvex setting are 3.9%, 0.7%, and 3.1%. In addition, with п¬Ғne-tuned hyperparameters in Table. 1, the personalized model of pFedMe beats others in all settings while the global model of pFedMe only performs better than FedAvg. From the experimental results, when the data among clients are non-i.i.d, both pFedMe and Per-Avg gain higher testing accuracy than FedAvg as they allow the global model to be personalized for a speciп¬Ғc client. However, by optimizing the personalized model approximately with multiple gradient updates and avoiding computing the Hessian matrix, the personalized model of pFedMe is more advantageous than Per-FedAvg in terms of the convergence rate and the computation complexity. 6 Conclusion In this paper, we propose pFedMe as a personalized FL algorithm that can adapt to the statistical diversity issue to improve the FL performance. Our approach makes use of the Moreau envelope function which helps decompose the personalized model optimization from global model learning, which allows pFedMe to update the global model similarly to FedAvg, yet in parallel to optimize the personalized model w.r.t each clientвҖҷs local data distribution. Theoretical results show thatpFedMe 10can achieve the state-of-the-art convergence speedup rate. Experimental results demonstrate that pFedMe outperforms the vanilla FedAvg and the meta-learning based personalized FL algorithm Per-FedAvg in both convex and non-convex settings, using both real and synthetic datasets. Finally, the degree to which personalization becomes provably useful is a topic of experimental research, as parameters will need to be adapted to each dataset and federated setting. One notable commercial FL usage, which has proved successful in recent years, is in the next-character prediction task on mobile devices. However, we believe this technology promises many more breakthroughs in a number of п¬Ғelds in the near future with the help of personalized FL models. In health care, for example, common causes of a disease can be identiп¬Ғed from many patients without the need to have access to their raw data. The development of capable personalized models helps build better predictors on patientsвҖҷ conditions, allowing for faster, more efп¬Ғcient diagnosis and treatment. As much as FL promises, it also comes with a number of challenges. First, an important societal requirement when deploying such technique is that the server must explain which clientsвҖҷ data will be participated and which will not. The explainability and interpretability of a system are necessary for the sake of public understanding and making informed consent. Second, to successfully preserve privacy, FL has to overcome malicious actors who possibly interfere in the training process during communication. The malicious behaviors include stealing personalized models from the server, perform adversarial attacks such as changing a personalized model on some examples while remaining a good performance on average, and attempt to alter the model. Finally, an effective and unbiased FL system must be aware that data and computational power among clients can be extremely uneven in practice and, therefore, must ensure that the contribution of each client to the global model is adjusted to its level of distribution. These challenges help necessitate future research in decentralized learning in general and personalized FL in particular. reference_research_study_list.title: Calibrating noise to sensitivity in private data analysis

new_method.method:
Title: PACER-FL вҖ” Auditable SecAgg-tree noise + residual DP updates with control variates + DP gating + FO-aware linear compression to tighten the privacyвҖ“efficiency frontier in cross-device FL

Motivation
- Problem and why now: Production FL (keyboards, health, speech) increasingly requires user-level DP without trusting the server while staying within mobile communication/latency budgets. Current deployments largely rely on DP-SGD-style i.i.d. Gaussian noise with uniform clipping. This induces bias, accumulates as вҲҡT across rounds, and interacts poorly with non-IID data and partial participation, widening the privacyвҖ“utilityвҖ“efficiency gap.
- Gaps in the literature:
  вҖў DP-FTRL/tree aggregation gives strictly better privacyвҖ“utility scaling via correlated noise, but is usually implemented server-side, is hard to audit, and is not integrated with SecAgg or compression.
  вҖў Private FO with autotuned compression (Adapt Norm) assumes independent Gaussian noise and mean-estimation objectives; it does not marry correlated noise with compression or participation decisions, nor does it coordinate with FL optimization signals.
  вҖў Personalization methods (PPSGD, CENTAUR) improve utility but still assume independent noise, fixed/global clipping, and lack DP-safe gating/participation control and co-tuning with SecAgg-compatible compression.
  вҖў Accountants (FS-RDP, PRV) are available but not operationalized for a pipeline that composes SecAgg-compatible tree-correlated Gaussians, DP gating, and DP quantile clipping under fixed-size sampling in cross-device FL.
- Opportunity: Provide a single, auditable, deployable stack that (i) synthesizes DP-FTRL-class tree-correlated Gaussian noise on clients under SecAgg with public verifiability, (ii) shrinks per-user sensitivity through residualized/control-variate updates, and (iii) privately gates participation and autotunes SecAgg-compatible linear compression so that compression error matches the DP noise floor round-by-round. This directly advances the federated learning privacyвҖ“efficiency tradeoff (гғ•гӮ§гғҮгғ¬гғјгғҶгғғгғүеӯҰзҝ’гҒ®гғ—гғ©гӮӨгғҗгӮ·гғјеҠ№зҺҮгғҲгғ¬гғјгғүгӮӘгғ•гҒ®ж”№е–„) while closing adoption blockers (auditability, systems integration).

Methodology
Core idea: PACER-FL co-designs four SecAgg-compatible components and unifies their privacy accounting.

A) DDG-TREE: Client-synthesized, verifiable tree-correlated noise compatible with SecAgg and compression
- Public randomness and audit: For each round and each node of a binary aggregation tree, the server publishes seeds and target variances in a transparency log (e.g., VRF-derived seeds, signed; Merkle-rooted). No private server-side noise.
- Client-side shares: Each selected client deterministically samples discrete-Gaussian (DDG) shares per tree node from the published seeds. Under SecAgg, shares add to the target tree noise (matching DP-FTRL covariance). Calibrate shares to a pre-declared minimum cohort L_min so realized noise вүҘ target even when L вүҘ L_min; if L < L_min, add a pre-committed rescue noise drawn from the same seeds (auditable post-processing).
- Sketch-domain shaping: Clients send S_t r_i^t (linear sketch; CountSketch/sparse JL) and add DDG-TREE shares directly in sketch space so that after unsketching the aggregate noise covariance equals the intended DP-FTRL tree covariance projected through S_t^T S_t. This preserves SecAgg linearity and aligns privatization with compression.
- Dropouts/restarts: Support DP-FTRL NoTreeRestart/SometimesRestart/TreeRestart schedules; publish schedule and L_min. Auditors can recompute all realized aggregate noise from logs and cohort sizes to verify it meets/exceeds targets.

B) Residual DP updates with DIANA-style control variates to reduce sensitivity
- Residualization: The server maintains a global control variate c_t (e.g., momentum-smoothed aggregate). Client i tracks h_i^t and sends only the residual r_i^t = g_i^t вҲ’ h_i^t, with h_i^{t+1} вҶҗ (1вҲ’Оұ) h_i^t + Оұ c_t. Residual norms shrink over training, enabling tighter per-layer clipping and less DP noise for a fixed Оө.
- Client pipeline: r_i^t вҶ’ per-layer L2 clipping at C_res,t (set via DP quantiles below) вҶ’ sketch S_t r_i^t вҶ’ add DDG-TREE shares вҶ’ SecAgg upload. Server unsketches, updates c_t, and applies momentum/optimizer as usual.
- Utility intuition: Combining residualization with tree aggregation replaces вҲҡT noise growth by вҲҡlog T and scales sensitivity by E||r_i^t||/E||g_i^t||<1, improving SNR without extra privacy cost. This differs from PPSGD/CENTAUR by operating on residuals of the shared model update and by integrating correlated noise.

C) DP automation: private gating, private clipping, and FO-aware linear compression
- DP gating (AboveThreshold): Each client computes a scalar score s_i^t of update utility (e.g., s_i^t = max{cos(r_i^t, c_t), О»||r_i^t||}). A local AboveThreshold test with Laplace noise decides ON/OFF under a small budget ПҒ_gate per round. To hide participation, OFF clients still send masked zero-sketch plus DDG-TREE shares; SecAgg reveals only the sum. Compose ПҒ_gate via PRV.
- DP quantile clipping: Allocate a tiny budget ПҒ_clip to privately update per-layer quantiles of residual norms (e.g., 90th percentile via noisy sketches). Set C_res,t to the private quantile, keeping clipping tight with minimal bias and reduced noise magnitude.
- FO-aware autotuned compression: Choose sketch dimension m_t so that compression error вүҲ DP/statistical noise for that round. Use the Fundamental Price of SecAgg principle but adapt to residuals and correlated noise. Practical rule: m_t вүҲ Оә В· min{d, n_t^2 Оө_eff,t^2 В· M_t^2}, where n_t is cohort size, Оө_eff,t is the roundвҖҷs effective allocation from the accountant under the current tree schedule, M_t is a private estimate of ||Ој_res,t||/C_res,t from the previous round (obtained from the aggregate; no extra privacy cost), and ОәвүҘ1 is a safety factor. This extends Adapt Norm from independent noise/mean-estimation to correlated noise in FO and to DP-gated participation.

D) Unified privacy accounting and system integration
- Accounting: Compose (i) fixed-size sampling (FS-RDP) for cross-device cohorts, (ii) multi-step tree-correlated Gaussians (modeled via DP-FTRL RDP with restarts and composed using PRV), (iii) Laplace AboveThreshold gating, and (iv) DP quantiles. Default ОҙвүҲ1/N. Numerically optimize orders and track per-round Оө_eff,t to drive m_t and C_res,t.
- Integration (TFF/PyTorch-FL/Opacus):
  вҖў Server: publishes VRF seeds/tree schedule/sketch parameters; runs SecAgg; unsketches; updates optimizer and c_t; sets m_t and C_res,t from accountant feedback; logs cohort sizes and randomness.  
  вҖў Client: maintains h_i^t; computes r_i^t; runs DP quantile update locally; clips; sketches; adds DDG-TREE shares from public seeds; performs DP AboveThreshold gating; always uploads masked sketch+shares.
- Safety/fairness: Enforce a DP-throttled minimum ON rate per user or per stratum by adaptively relaxing thresholds within a small privacy budget. Publish privacy-preserving cohort statistics (e.g., stratified ON rates) for governance.

How PACER-FL differs from prior work
- DP-FTRL: Retains tree-correlated noise benefits but moves noise synthesis to clients with SecAgg compatibility and public audit, and aligns it with linear compression; prior DP-FTRL is server-side and not auditable nor compression-aware.
- Private FO with Adapt Norm: Generalizes autotuned compression from independent Gaussian noise/mean estimation to correlated noise in FL, couples it with residual sensitivity reduction and DP gating, and provides a single accountant covering all mechanisms.
- PPSGD/CENTAUR: Goes beyond personalization by shrinking sensitivity through residualized shared updates, introducing DP-safe participation gating, and using correlated noise, all under SecAgg and central-DP guarantees.
- FS-RDP/PRV: Operationalizes these accountants jointly for fixed-size sampling, tree correlation, AboveThreshold, and DP quantiles in one deployable pipeline.
- Fundamental Price of SecAgg: Builds on the DDG+sketch achievability but extends to multi-round correlated noise, residual updates, and DP-adaptive participation/compression, with end-to-end auditability.

Expected impact
- Better accuracy at fixed privacy: Residualization reduces sensitivity; tree-correlated noise replaces вҲҡT with вҲҡlog T growth; DP quantile clipping keeps C_res,t tight. Expect several-point accuracy gains over DP-FedAvg with i.i.d. noise at ОөвҲҲ[1,5], particularly under non-IID and late training.
- Lower communication without extra leakage: FO-aware sketching drives per-client bits toward the SecAgg fundamental price Г•(min{d, n^2 Оө^2}); DP gating suppresses low-value uploads while hiding ON/OFF under SecAgg, saving 20вҖ“50% uplink in challenging heterogeneity regimes.
- Trust and deployability: All DP noise is client-generated from public randomness; auditors can verify realized noise вүҘ targets end-to-end. Linear, SecAgg-compatible encoders integrate with production stacks; no shuffle or multi-server changes required.
- Fairness and robustness: DP gating with minimum participation constraints reduces systematic exclusion; residualization supports personalization-friendly behavior. Public logs enable external monitoring of participation equity and privacy budget use.
- Reusable infrastructure: Provides an accountant and auditable randomness ledger others can adopt. Establishes a general recipeвҖ”correlated-noise Г— residual sensitivity Г— DP automation under SecAggвҖ”for pushing the privacyвҖ“efficiency frontier across FL tasks and systems.

experimental_design.experiment_strategy:
PACER-FL: Three complementary experiments to validate privacyвҖ“utilityвҖ“efficiency, communication savings, and auditability/robustness.

Experiment 1 вҖ” UtilityвҖ“privacy gains from DDG-TREE + residualization + DP automation under non-IID data
Objective/Hypothesis
- Test that PACER-FL achieves higher accuracy at fixed user-level DP than i.i.d.-noise DP-FedAvg and server-side DP-FTRL, especially under non-IID and partial participation. Hypotheses: (H1) Tree-correlated noise + residualization gives вүҘ2вҖ“5 point accuracy gains at Оө in [1,5]; (H2) residuals shrink sensitivity (||r_i|| вүӘ ||g_i||) enabling tighter clipping and lower effective noise; (H3) вҲҡlog T noise growth is observed vs вҲҡT for i.i.d. noise.

Task, model, and FL setup
- Dataset: CIFAR-10 (torchvision), non-IID Dirichlet partition over clients with Оұ_dir=0.1. (Alternatively EMNIST-balanced; code paths similar.)
- Clients: N=10_000 simulated; per-round cohort size n_t=200 (fixed-size sampling); total rounds T=600; local epochs=1; batch size=64; client LR=0.05 (SGD + momentum 0.9); server optimizer: FedAvg + server momentum 0.9.
- Model: modest CNN (Conv-Conv-Pool-Conv-Conv-Pool-FC-FC), вүҲ1.3M params.
- Privacy target: user-level DP with Оҙ=1e-6. Global budget per run ОөвҲҲ{1.0, 2.0, 4.0}. Fixed-size sampling fraction q=n_t/N=0.02.

Methods compared
- Non-DP FedAvg upper bound (no noise, standard clipping off).
- Baseline A: DP-FedAvg + i.i.d. Gaussian server noise after SecAgg; uniform per-layer clipping C_t set by DP-quantile or fixed (best tuned).
- Baseline B: DP-FTRL (server-side tree-correlated Gaussian noise, NoTreeRestart; not auditable, no compression-alignment); same clipping strategy as A.
- PACER-FL (full): DDG-TREE (client-synthesized shares + audit), residualized DP updates with DIANA-style control variates, DP AboveThreshold gating with ПҒ_gate, DP quantile clipping with ПҒ_clip, FO-aware linear sketch compression sized by Оө_eff,t.
- Ablations: PACER w/o residualization; PACER w/o DP gating; PACER w/o FO-aware compression (fixed sketch size ~1/8 parameters); PACER w/o DP quantiles (fixed clipping).

Primary metrics
- Test accuracy vs rounds; final accuracy at matched Оө.
- Effective Оө from accountant vs target Оө (should not exceed target).
- Per-round SNR: ||Ој_res,t|| / sqrt(Var(noise along Ој_res,t)).
- Residual shrinkage: E[||r_i||] / E[||g_i||] over rounds; clipping utilization (fraction clipped).
- Noise growth: Fit slope of log RMS noise vs log T to validate вҲҡlog T vs вҲҡT.

Key implementation details
- SecAgg simulation: only linear aggregation is used; simulate by summing masked vectors; server only sees sums.
- DDG-TREE: binary tree with height вҢҲlog2 TвҢү; NoTreeRestart default; minimum cohort L_min published; rescue noise engaged if realized L<L_min.
- Residualization: client maintains h_i^t; server maintains control variate c_t (EMA of past aggregates); r_i^t=g_i^tвҲ’h_i^t; h update: h_i^{t+1}=(1вҲ’Оұ)h_i^t+Оұ c_t, with Оұ=0.2.
- DP gating: s_i^t = max(cos(r_i^t, c_t), О»||r_i^t||) with О»=0.1/C_res,t; Laplace(1/П„) noise; AboveThreshold vs public Оё_t, ПҒ_gate per round such that cumulative Оө_gate вүӨ 0.2 of budget; OFF-clients send zero-sketch plus DDG-TREE shares.
- DP quantile clipping: per-layer private P90 of ||r_i,layer|| with tiny ПҒ_clip such that cumulative Оө_clip вүӨ 0.2 of budget; set C_res,t to private quantile; compose via accountant.
- FO-aware compression: CountSketch with m_t chosen so expected sketch-induced MSE вүҲ DP noise floor along dominant directions; m_t вүҲ ОәВ·min{d, (n_tВ·Оө_eff,tВ·M_t)^2}, Оә=1.2, M_t from previous round aggregate.
- Accounting: PRV/PLD or RDP composition for fixed-size sampling, multi-step tree Gaussians (DP-FTRL), Laplace gating, and DP quantiles; Оҙ=1e-6; numerically solve for per-round Gaussian scales to meet target Оө by round T.

Success criteria and analysis
- PACER-FL dominates baselines on accuracy at matched Оө across Оө grid; gains largest at ОөвүӨ2 and later rounds.
- Residual norms drop вүҘ2Г— from early to mid training; clipping percent remains 10вҖ“20%.
- Auditor reconstruction of noise shows realized covariance вүҘ target; вҲҡlog T growth confirmed.

Python skeletons (core pieces)
- Tree schedule and client share synthesis
  def tree_nodes_for_round(t):
      # return list of binary-tree node ids active at round t (1-indexed tree)
      # classic DP-FTRL schedule: nodes correspond to 1-bits in t
      nodes=[]; k=0; x=t
      while x>0:
          if x & 1: nodes.append((k, (t>>k)<<k))  # (depth, node_start_round)
          k+=1; x >>=1
      return nodes

  def ddg_int_gaussian(shape, sigma, rng):
      # Discrete Gaussian via rounded continuous Gaussian (audit-friendly)
      return np.rint(rng.normal(0.0, sigma, size=shape)).astype(np.int64)

  def client_ddg_tree_shares(round_t, sketch_dim, seeds, target_var_per_node, L_min, cohort_size):
      # seeds: dict[(depth,node_start)] -> seed int
      # target_var_per_node: dict[(depth,node_start)] -> sigma_node^2 in sketch space
      rngs={k: np.random.default_rng(seeds[k]) for k in seeds}
      shares=np.zeros(sketch_dim, dtype=np.int64)
      for node in tree_nodes_for_round(round_t):
          sigma=np.sqrt(target_var_per_node[node])
          # scale per-user share so that sum over cohort approximates target variance
          # with calibration to L_min to ensure realized variance >= target when L>=L_min
          scale = np.sqrt(max(L_min, cohort_size))
          z = ddg_int_gaussian(sketch_dim, sigma/scale, rngs[node])
          shares += z
      return shares

- CountSketch
  class CountSketch:
      def __init__(self, d, m, seed):
          rng=np.random.default_rng(seed)
          self.h = rng.integers(0, m, size=d)        # bucket indices
          self.s = rng.choice([-1,1], size=d)        # signs
          self.m = m
      def S(self, x):
          y=np.zeros(self.m, dtype=np.float32)
          np.add.at(y, self.h, self.s * x)
          return y
      def unS(self, y):
          # simple unsketch: divide bucket by counts, then scatter back with sign
          counts=np.bincount(self.h, minlength=self.m).astype(np.float32)
          inv_counts=np.where(counts>0, 1.0/counts, 0.0)
          bucket_vals = y * inv_counts
          return bucket_vals[self.h] * self.s

- DP gating (AboveThreshold)
  def dp_gate(score, theta, sensitivity, eps):
      b = sensitivity/eps  # Laplace scale
      noisy_score = score + np.random.laplace(0.0, b)
      return noisy_score >= theta

- FO-aware sketch dimension
  def choose_m_t(d, n_t, eps_eff_t, M_t, kappa=1.2):
      m_est = int(kappa * min(d, max(8, (n_t * eps_eff_t * M_t)**2)))
      return max(16, min(d, m_est))

- Accountant placeholder (use google dp-accounting or autodp in practice)
  class Accountant:
      def __init__(self, delta): self.delta=delta
      def add_tree_gaussian(self, sigma_vec): pass
      def add_laplace(self, eps): pass
      def get_epsilon(self): return 2.0  # placeholder


Experiment 2 вҖ” CommunicationвҖ“efficiency frontier: FO-aware compression + DP gating vs classic compressors at fixed DP
Objective/Hypothesis
- Show PACER-FL achieves substantial uplink reduction (20вҖ“50%) at fixed Оө without utility loss by aligning sketch error to DP noise and suppressing low-value updates privately.

Task and setup
- Dataset/model: EMNIST (Balanced), CNN (~1.0M params) to emphasize dimensionality. N=5_000 clients; n_t=100; T=500; Оұ_dir=0.1 non-IID. Same optimizers as Exp.1.
- Privacy: Оө=2, Оҙ=1e-6 at horizon; same accounting as Exp.1.

Methods compared
- Baseline C: DP-FedAvg + i.i.d. Gaussian, QSGD-8bit quantization.
- Baseline D: DP-FedAvg + i.i.d. Gaussian, Top-k sparsification with error feedback (k=1%).
- Baseline E: Private FO with Adapt Norm (independent noise assumption) + its suggested autotuned compression.
- PACER-FL full (FO-aware sketch + DDG-TREE + residual + DP gating).
- PACER-wo-gate (to isolate gating savings).

Metrics
- Uplink bits/client/round and total; wall-clock (simulated) per round.
- Test accuracy at horizon and AUC of accuracy-vs-bits curve.
- Fraction of OFF gated clients per round; ON-rate fairness across strata (by dominant class per client or random device tier strata).
- Alignment check: ratio of compression MSE to DP noise MSE stays in [0.7, 1.3].

Implementation specifics
- Communication counting: CountSketch m_t integers (32-bit) + seed ids; include constant SecAgg mask overhead per client; for baselines, count quantized values + indices (for Top-k) + seeds.
- DP gating: choose Оё_t so expected ON-rateвүҲ0.6 early, 0.4 mid, 0.3 late; enforce DP-throttled minimum ON-rate 0.2 via adaptive Оё_t within Оө_gate budget.
- FO alignment: compute M_t from previous round aggregate norm normalized by C_res,t; choose m_t by choose_m_t; verify alignment metric.

Success criteria
- PACER-FL achieves вүҘ25% less uplink than best baseline at same accuracy and Оө; OFF/ON indistinguishable to server view (only sums observed); compression error tracks DP noise.

Python skeletons (selected)
- Bits accounting for CountSketch
  def bits_per_client_countsketch(m, value_bits=32, seed_bits=64, extra_overhead_bits=256):
      return m*value_bits + seed_bits + extra_overhead_bits

- Alignment probe
  def estimate_dp_noise_mse(sigma_eff, d):
      return d * sigma_eff**2
  def estimate_sketch_mse(x, sketch: CountSketch):
      # empirical via resketch
      y = sketch.S(x)
      x_hat = sketch.unS(y)
      return float(np.mean((x_hat - x)**2))


Experiment 3 вҖ” Auditability and robustness under dropouts, L_min calibration, and tree restarts
Objective/Hypothesis
- Validate that client-synthesized DDG-TREE noise is publicly auditable and meets/exceeds target even with heavy dropouts and variable cohort sizes; assess robustness of utility under NoTreeRestart/SometimesRestart/TreeRestart and fairness of DP gating.

Setup
- Use CIFAR-10 setup from Exp.1 at Оө=2; run three stress regimes:
  R1: Mild dropouts p=0.2; cohort n_t held fixed (server resamples if needed).
  R2: Heavy dropouts p=0.6; realized cohort can drop below L_min 10% of rounds (trigger rescue noise).
  R3: Bursty participation: alternating phases with p in {0.2, 0.8}; include SometimesRestart schedule.
- Publish per-round log: VRF-like seeds, target variances per node, L_min, realized cohort size, sketch params.

Auditor procedures and metrics
- Reconstruct aggregate noise in sketch space from public seeds and realized cohort sizes, then unsketch to model space; compute empirical covariance across repeated runs; verify PSD and вүҘ target covariance along all tree directions (within statistical tolerance).
- Count number of rescue-noise activations; confirm post-processed covariance meets target in rounds with L<L_min.
- Accuracy degradation vs NoTreeRestart in R1; robustness gap вүӨ1.5 points in R2/R3.
- Fairness: track private ON-rate constraints per stratum; show min-ON enforcement lifts worst-stratum ON rate to вүҘ0.2 with вүӨ0.3Оө extra budget; accuracy disparity across strata does not widen.

Python skeletons (auditor core)
- Auditor noise reconstruction
  def auditor_reconstruct_noise(round_t, sketch_dim, seeds, target_var_per_node, L_min, realized_L):
      # Expected aggregate share sum is 0 mean; variance per node equals target_var when L>=L_min
      rngs={k: np.random.default_rng(seeds[k]) for k in seeds}
      agg=np.zeros(sketch_dim, dtype=np.int64)
      for node in tree_nodes_for_round(round_t):
          sigma=np.sqrt(target_var_per_node[node])
          scale=np.sqrt(max(L_min, realized_L))
          # Sum of realized_L discrete Gaussians with per-user sigma/scale equals target sigma
          z = ddg_int_gaussian(sketch_dim, sigma, rngs[node])  # direct aggregate sample for audit
          agg += z
      return agg

- Restart schedules
  def tree_restart_schedule(T, mode="NoTreeRestart"):
      # returns mapping round->set of active nodes; implement SometimesRestart/TreeRestart by resetting nodes
      # placeholder: implement according to DP-FTRL specs
      pass

Reproducibility and shared components for all experiments
- Libraries: PyTorch, NumPy, torchvision; sketching via custom CountSketch; accounting via google dp-accounting (privacy_loss) or autodp; reproducible RNG seeds set at start.
- Non-IID split: Dirichlet(Оұ) over labels per client; ensure each client has at least 20 samples by rejection.
- Training loop structure: server publishes seeds/schedule and m_t, C_res,t; clients compute r_i^t, DP-gate, clip, sketch, add DDG-TREE shares, send; server SecAgg-sums, unsketches, updates model and c_t; accountant updates Оө_eff,t; logs written.
- Hyperparameter sweeps: small grids over clipping multipliers, Оұ (control-variate update), gating Оё_t schedule; early stopping by validation.
- Statistical reporting: 3 seeds per condition; report meanВұ95% CI.

Deliverables
- Plots: accuracy vs Оө; accuracy vs bits; SNR and residual-norm trajectories; compression/DP-noise alignment ratio; auditor covariance checks; fairness ON-rate by strata.
- Public randomness and accounting logs + auditor script to independently verify noise guarantees.
experimental_design.experiment_details:
PACER-FL experiments: end-to-end experimental design, implementation details, and code skeletons (PyTorch)

This document details how to run the three PACER-FL experiments with auditable DDG-TREE noise, residual DP updates with control variates, DP gating + DP quantile clipping, and FO-aware linear compression. The plan emphasizes reproducibility, unified accounting, and minimal from-scratch code. Where a capability is not available in a standard library, we provide short, auditable utilities with clear interfaces.

Prerequisites (all experiments)
- Python packages
  вҖў torch, torchvision, torchaudio
  вҖў numpy, scipy, pandas, pillow, tqdm
  вҖў dp-accounting (Google DP accounting; pip install dp-accounting)
  вҖў autodp (as a fallback for RDP/PRV; pip install autodp)
  вҖў diffprivlib (IBM DP tools for Laplace and DP histograms; pip install diffprivlib)
  вҖў pyyaml or jsonlines (for logs)
  вҖў matplotlib or seaborn (plots)

- Reproducibility
  вҖў Fix torch, numpy RNG seeds per run and per-experiment seeds for cohort sampling, tree seeds, and sketch seeds.
  вҖў Log all randomness used for DDG-TREE (per-node VRF-like seeds), sketch seeds, and cohort sizes in a transparency ledger (JSONL), signed if possible.

Shared utilities used by all experiments
- Non-IID partitioning (Dirichlet Оұ)
- CNN model
- CountSketch class and unsketch
- Tree schedule and per-round node set
- Client-side DDG-TREE share synthesis (discrete Gaussians, audit-friendly)
- DP gating (AboveThreshold with Laplace)
- DP quantile clipping via DP histogram aggregation
- FO-aware sketch dimension selection
- Unified privacy accountant wrapper (dp-accounting + autodp fallback)
- SecAgg simulation (sum-only interface; OFF clients send zero-sketch + noise shares)
- Logging (transparency ledger + training metrics)

Code: core shared components

1) Non-IID split (Dirichlet) and data loading

import numpy as np, torch, random
from torch.utils.data import DataLoader, Subset
from torchvision import datasets, transforms

def dirichlet_noniid_split(labels, num_clients, alpha=0.1, min_size=20, seed=0):
    rng = np.random.default_rng(seed)
    K = int(labels.max()) + 1
    idx_by_class = [np.where(labels == k)[0] for k in range(K)]
    for idx in idx_by_class: rng.shuffle(idx)
    proportions = rng.dirichlet(alpha=np.ones(num_clients), size=K)  # K x num_clients
    client_indices = [[] for _ in range(num_clients)]
    for k in range(K):
        idx = idx_by_class[k]
        splits = (np.cumsum(np.floor(proportions[k] / proportions[k].sum() * len(idx))).astype(int))
        splits = np.clip(splits, 0, len(idx))
        splits = np.unique(np.concatenate([[0], splits, [len(idx)]]))
        for c in range(num_clients):
            client_indices[c].extend(idx[splits[c]:splits[c+1]])
    # Enforce min_size by borrowing across neighbors if needed
    for c in range(num_clients):
        if len(client_indices[c]) < min_size:
            # simple top-up: sample random indices from global pool not already used; for simulation only
            needed = min_size - len(client_indices[c])
            pool = np.setdiff1d(np.arange(len(labels)), np.array(client_indices[c], dtype=int), assume_unique=False)
            client_indices[c].extend(rng.choice(pool, size=needed, replace=False).tolist())
    return [np.array(sorted(list(set(idx))), dtype=int) for idx in client_indices]

2) Model

import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F

class SmallCIFAR10CNN(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, num_classes=10):
        super().__init__()
        self.conv1 = nn.Conv2d(3, 32, 3, padding=1)
        self.conv2 = nn.Conv2d(32, 64, 3, padding=1)
        self.pool = nn.MaxPool2d(2,2)
        self.conv3 = nn.Conv2d(64, 128, 3, padding=1)
        self.conv4 = nn.Conv2d(128, 128, 3, padding=1)
        self.fc1 = nn.Linear(8*8*128, 256)
        self.fc2 = nn.Linear(256, num_classes)
    def forward(self, x):
        x = F.relu(self.conv1(x))
        x = F.relu(self.conv2(x))
        x = self.pool(x)
        x = F.relu(self.conv3(x))
        x = F.relu(self.conv4(x))
        x = self.pool(x)
        x = torch.flatten(x, 1)
        x = F.relu(self.fc1(x))
        x = self.fc2(x)
        return x

3) CountSketch and FO-aware m_t

import numpy as np

class CountSketch:
    def __init__(self, d, m, seed=0):
        rng = np.random.default_rng(seed)
        self.h = rng.integers(0, m, size=d)
        self.s = rng.choice([-1, 1], size=d)
        self.m = m
        self.d = d
    def S(self, x: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
        y = np.zeros(self.m, dtype=np.float32)
        np.add.at(y, self.h, self.s * x)
        return y
    def unS(self, y: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
        counts = np.bincount(self.h, minlength=self.m).astype(np.float32)
        inv = np.where(counts > 0, 1.0 / counts, 0.0)
        bucket_vals = y * inv
        return bucket_vals[self.h] * self.s

def choose_m_t(d, n_t, eps_eff_t, M_t, kappa=1.2):
    m_est = int(kappa * min(d, max(8, (n_t * eps_eff_t * max(M_t, 1e-6))**2)))
    return int(max(16, min(d, m_est)))

4) Tree schedule and DDG-TREE share synthesis

# Nodes are keyed by (depth, start_round). NoTreeRestart by default.
def tree_nodes_for_round(t: int):
    nodes = []
    k, x = 0, t
    while x > 0:
        if x & 1:
            nodes.append((k, (t >> k) << k))
        k += 1
        x >>= 1
    return nodes

def ddg_int_gaussian(shape, sigma, rng):
    return np.rint(rng.normal(0.0, sigma, size=shape)).astype(np.int64)

# Each client creates integer-valued shares per active node, in sketch space
# target_var_per_node is variance of the AGGREGATE noise per node in sketch space

def client_ddg_tree_shares(round_t, sketch_dim, seeds, target_var_per_node, L_min, cohort_size):
    rngs = {k: np.random.default_rng(seeds[k]) for k in seeds}
    shares = np.zeros(sketch_dim, dtype=np.int64)
    for node in tree_nodes_for_round(round_t):
        sigma = np.sqrt(target_var_per_node[node])
        scale = np.sqrt(max(L_min, cohort_size))
        z = ddg_int_gaussian(sketch_dim, sigma/scale, rngs[node])
        shares += z
    return shares

# Auditor-side reconstruction of aggregate noise (sketch domain)
def auditor_reconstruct_noise(round_t, sketch_dim, seeds, target_var_per_node, L_min, realized_L):
    rngs = {k: np.random.default_rng(seeds[k]) for k in seeds}
    agg = np.zeros(sketch_dim, dtype=np.int64)
    for node in tree_nodes_for_round(round_t):
        sigma = np.sqrt(target_var_per_node[node])
        z = ddg_int_gaussian(sketch_dim, sigma, rngs[node])
        agg += z
    return agg

5) DP gating (AboveThreshold) and Laplace utilities

from diffprivlib.mechanisms import Laplace as DPLaplace

def dp_gate(score: float, theta: float, sensitivity: float, eps: float, rng=None) -> bool:
    mech = DPLaplace(epsilon=max(eps, 1e-8), sensitivity=max(sensitivity, 1e-12), random_state=rng)
    noisy_score = mech.randomise(score)
    return noisy_score >= theta

6) DP quantile clipping via DP histogram aggregation

from diffprivlib.tools import histogram as dp_histogram

def private_quantile_from_histogram(norms: np.ndarray, q=0.9, clip_max=10.0, eps=0.05, bins=50, rng=None):
    # norms are non-negative residual norms (per-layer or whole vector, per-client)
    # Build histogram [0, clip_max]
    counts, edges = dp_histogram(norms, range=(0.0, clip_max), bins=bins, epsilon=eps, random_state=rng)
    cdf = np.cumsum(counts) / max(np.sum(counts), 1e-9)
    idx = np.searchsorted(cdf, q)
    idx = min(max(idx, 0), len(edges) - 2)
    return float(edges[idx + 1])

7) Unified privacy accounting wrapper

# We rely on dp_accounting for Gaussian/Laplace and fixed-size sampling; for tree-correlated Gaussians we approximate by per-round effective Gaussian steps with RDP, and we track separate events for gating and quantiles with pure epsilon composition.

from dp_accounting import privacy_loss_distribution as pld
from dp_accounting import rdp as rdp_mod

class Accountant:
    def __init__(self, delta=1e-6):
        self.delta = delta
        self.events = []
    def add_gaussian_event(self, noise_multiplier, sampling_probability):
        # Gaussian mechanism under Poisson subsampling (approx) using RDP
        # If using fixed-size sampling, approximate with Poisson q for accounting
        q = sampling_probability
        orders = np.concatenate([np.linspace(1.25, 64, 200), np.array([128, 256])])
        rdp = rdp_mod.compute_rdp(q=q, noise_multiplier=noise_multiplier, steps=1, orders=orders)
        self.events.append(("rdp", rdp, orders))
    def add_laplace_pure_eps(self, eps):
        self.events.append(("pure_eps", eps))
    def get_epsilon(self):
        # Compose RDP events + pure eps via PLD approximation; fallback to simple composition
        total_rdp = None; orders = None; pure_eps = 0.0
        for kind, payload, *rest in self.events:
            if kind == "rdp":
                rdp, orders = payload, rest[0]
                if total_rdp is None:
                    total_rdp = rdp.copy()
                else:
                    total_rdp += rdp
            elif kind == "pure_eps":
                pure_eps += payload
        eps_rdp = 0.0
        if total_rdp is not None:
            eps_rdp = rdp_mod.get_privacy_spent(orders=orders, rdp=total_rdp, delta=self.delta)[0]
        return eps_rdp + pure_eps
    def reset(self): self.events = []

# Note: For DP-FTRL/tree-correlated noise, we map the per-round aggregate to an equivalent Gaussian step with an effective noise multiplier sigma_eff_t that matches the variance along the released aggregate direction; this is conservative.

8) Bits accounting for CountSketch

def bits_per_client_countsketch(m, value_bits=32, seed_bits=64, extra_overhead_bits=256):
    return m * value_bits + seed_bits + extra_overhead_bits

9) Transparency ledger format (JSONL)

import json, os

def write_ledger_record(path, record_dict):
    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(path), exist_ok=True)
    with open(path, "a") as f:
        f.write(json.dumps(record_dict) + "\n")

Ledger entries per round t:
{
  "round": t,
  "vrf_seeds": {"(depth,start)": seed_int, ...},
  "target_var_per_node": {"(depth,start)": var_float, ...},
  "L_min": int,
  "realized_cohort": int,
  "sketch_seed": int,
  "sketch_m": int,
  "clip_quantiles": {"layer_name": C_res_value, ...},
  "gate_theta": float,
  "eps_eff_round": float,
  "eps_gate_round": float,
  "eps_clip_round": float,
  "restart_mode": "NoTreeRestart|SometimesRestart|TreeRestart"
}

Experiment 1 вҖ” UtilityвҖ“privacy gains under non-IID with DDG-TREE + residualization + DP automation

Objective and hypotheses
- H1: PACER-FL achieves 2вҖ“5 point higher accuracy vs DP-FedAvg with i.i.d. Gaussian and vs server-side DP-FTRL at Оө in {1,2,4} under non-IID data.
- H2: Residualization shrinks sensitivity: mean residual norm substantially below full gradient norm; clipping utilization 10вҖ“20%.
- H3: Aggregate noise growth follows вҲҡlog T (tree) versus вҲҡT (i.i.d.), visible in the slope of log RMS noise vs log T.

Task, data, and model
- Dataset: CIFAR-10 with standard train/test splits; transforms: random crop + flip for train; normalization.
- Non-IID: Dirichlet Оұ=0.1 across N=10,000 clients, minimum 20 samples/client.
- Model: SmallCIFAR10CNN (~1.3M params), as above.

Federated setup
- Rounds T=600, fixed-size cohort per round n_t=200 (sampling fraction q=0.02).
- Local epochs=1, batch size=64, client optimizer SGD(lr=0.05, momentum=0.9).
- Server optimizer: FedAvg with server momentum 0.9; optionally Nesterov off.

Privacy targets and allocation
- Оҙ=1e-6, Оө вҲҲ {1.0, 2.0, 4.0} at T.
- Budget split (guideline): Оө_tree вүҲ 0.6Оө, Оө_gate вүҲ 0.2Оө, Оө_clip вүҲ 0.2Оө. Convert to per-round allocations based on T and restart schedule (NoTreeRestart by default). Gate and clip use pure-epsilon composition (Laplace and DP histogram). Tree noise accounted via RDP with effective noise multipliers per round.

Compared methods
- Non-DP FedAvg (upper bound; no clipping, no noise).
- Baseline A: DP-FedAvg + i.i.d. Gaussian at server; per-layer clipping fixed or tuned DP-quantile (best of both).
- Baseline B: Server-side DP-FTRL (tree-correlated Gaussian), no compression alignment, non-auditable.
- PACER-FL full: DDG-TREE on clients (auditable), residualized DP updates with DIANA-style control variates, DP AboveThreshold gating, DP quantile clipping, FO-aware CountSketch compression.
- Ablations: remove one module at a time: residualization, gating, FO-aware compression, DP quantiles.

Core implementation details
- Residualization/control variates
  вҖў Server maintains c_t (EMA of previous aggregates): c_t = (1вҲ’ОІ)c_{tвҲ’1} + ОІ gМ„_{tвҲ’1}, ОІвүҲ0.1вҖ“0.2.
  вҖў Client i tracks h_i^t, updated with c_t: h_i^{t+1} = (1вҲ’Оұ) h_i^t + Оұ c_t, Оұ=0.2.
  вҖў Client computes local gradient/update g_i^t and residual r_i^t = g_i^t вҲ’ h_i^t.

- Per-layer DP quantile clipping
  вҖў Each client computes per-layer residual L2 norms; contributes to DP histogram using diffprivlib per-round with Оө_clip_round.
  вҖў Server sets C_res,t[layer] to the 90th percentile from the DP histogram; clients clip residuals per layer to these thresholds next round.

- DP gating (AboveThreshold)
  вҖў Score s_i^t = max{cos(r_i^t, c_t), О»||r_i^t||} with О»=0.1/C_res,t_avg.
  вҖў Gate decision: ON if Laplace-noised s_i^t вүҘ Оё_t; else OFF. Оё_t is a public threshold schedule aiming for early ONвүҲ0.6, midвүҲ0.5, lateвүҲ0.4 while ensuring min per-user ONвүҘ0.2 via DP-throttled adjustments.
  вҖў OFF clients still upload zero sketch + DDG-TREE shares, preserving indistinguishability under SecAgg.

- FO-aware CountSketch compression
  вҖў Sketch dimension m_t chosen to align compression MSE to DP noise MSE: m_t вүҲ Оә В· min{d, (n_t Оө_eff,t M_t)^2}, Оә=1.2.
  вҖў M_t estimated as ||ОјМӮ_res,tвҲ’1|| / CМ„_res,tвҲ’1 from the previous round aggregate (no extra privacy cost, as it is a function of the released aggregate).

- DDG-TREE noise
  вҖў Binary tree of height вҢҲlog2 TвҢү; NoTreeRestart initially.
  вҖў Server publishes per-node seeds and target variance in sketch space (target_var_per_node[node]).
  вҖў Clients add per-node DDG discrete Gaussian shares directly in sketch space.
  вҖў L_min calibration: variance is calibrated to be вүҘ target for any realized cohort L вүҘ L_min; if L < L_min, server adds pre-committed rescue noise (auditable).

- Accounting
  вҖў Per round: add an effective Gaussian event with multiplier Пғ_eff_t to the Accountant for the aggregate under fixed-size sampling (approximated as Poisson q=0.02 for RDP). Also add pure-eps events for gating and clipping.
  вҖў Numerically adjust Пғ_eff_t schedule so that total Оө at round T matches target (bisection on a global scale factor).

End-to-end training loop skeleton

import torch, numpy as np
from torch.utils.data import DataLoader
from tqdm import trange

# Helpers to flatten/unflatten model params

def flatten_params(model):
    return torch.cat([p.detach().reshape(-1) for p in model.parameters()])

def assign_params(model, vec):
    offset = 0
    for p in model.parameters():
        num = p.numel()
        p.data.copy_(vec[offset:offset+num].reshape(p.shape))
        offset += num

# Client local step returning residual r_i

def client_compute_residual(model, data_loader, device, h_i_vec, c_t_vec, lr=0.05, momentum=0.9):
    local_model = SmallCIFAR10CNN().to(device)
    local_model.load_state_dict(model.state_dict())
    opt = torch.optim.SGD(local_model.parameters(), lr=lr, momentum=momentum)
    local_model.train()
    for xb, yb in data_loader:
        xb, yb = xb.to(device), yb.to(device)
        opt.zero_grad()
        logits = local_model(xb)
        loss = torch.nn.functional.cross_entropy(logits, yb)
        loss.backward()
        opt.step()
        break  # 1 epoch with batch_size as provided (simulate partial epoch if needed)
    g_i_vec = (flatten_params(local_model) - flatten_params(model)).detach().cpu().numpy()
    r_i = g_i_vec - h_i_vec
    # Also return per-layer norms if needed for DP hist
    return r_i

# Main server loop

def run_pacer_fl(exp_cfg):
    device = torch.device("cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")
    # Data
    transform_train = transforms.Compose([
        transforms.RandomCrop(32, padding=4), transforms.RandomHorizontalFlip(),
        transforms.ToTensor(), transforms.Normalize((0.4914,0.4822,0.4465), (0.2023,0.1994,0.2010))
    ])
    transform_test = transforms.Compose([
        transforms.ToTensor(), transforms.Normalize((0.4914,0.4822,0.4465), (0.2023,0.1994,0.2010))
    ])
    trainset = datasets.CIFAR10(root=exp_cfg["data_root"], train=True, download=True, transform=transform_train)
    testset = datasets.CIFAR10(root=exp_cfg["data_root"], train=False, download=True, transform=transform_test)
    labels = np.array(trainset.targets)
    client_indices = dirichlet_noniid_split(labels, num_clients=exp_cfg["N"], alpha=exp_cfg["alpha_dir"], seed=exp_cfg["seed"]) 

    # Server state
    global_model = SmallCIFAR10CNN().to(device)
    d = sum(p.numel() for p in global_model.parameters())
    c_t_vec = np.zeros(d, dtype=np.float32)
    beta = exp_cfg.get("beta", 0.1)
    alpha_cv = exp_cfg.get("alpha_cv", 0.2)
    accountant = Accountant(delta=exp_cfg["delta"]) 
    L_min = exp_cfg.get("L_min", int(exp_cfg["n_t"]*0.8))

    # Clipping and gating configs
    eps_total = exp_cfg["eps"]
    eps_tree = 0.6 * eps_total
    eps_gate_total = 0.2 * eps_total
    eps_clip_total = 0.2 * eps_total
    eps_gate_round = eps_gate_total / exp_cfg["T"]
    eps_clip_round = eps_clip_total / exp_cfg["T"]

    # Tree seeds and target variances per node (initialized and then scaled)
    # Start with unit variances per node in sketch space; scale globally later to hit eps_tree
    target_var_per_node = {}
    vrf_seeds = {}
    max_depth = int(np.ceil(np.log2(exp_cfg["T"]+1)))
    rng = np.random.default_rng(exp_cfg["seed"]) 
    for t in range(1, exp_cfg["T"]+1):
        for node in tree_nodes_for_round(t):
            if node not in vrf_seeds:
                vrf_seeds[node] = int(rng.integers(1, 2**31-1))
            if node not in target_var_per_node:
                target_var_per_node[node] = 1.0  # provisional; scale later

    # Initial sketch dim and sketched noise calibration
    n_t = exp_cfg["n_t"]; q = n_t / exp_cfg["N"]
    eps_eff_round = eps_tree / exp_cfg["T"]
    # Back-of-envelope: set global noise multiplier to meet eps_tree via RDP for Gaussian; calibrate scale
    # We search a scalar s such that composed epsilon вүҲ eps_tree.
    def epsilon_for_scale(s):
        acc = Accountant(delta=exp_cfg["delta"])
        for _ in range(exp_cfg["T"]):
            acc.add_gaussian_event(noise_multiplier=s, sampling_probability=q)
        return acc.get_epsilon()
    lo, hi = 0.1, 50.0
    for _ in range(30):
        mid = (lo+hi)/2
        if epsilon_for_scale(mid) > eps_tree: lo = mid
        else: hi = mid
    sigma_eff_global = hi

    # FO-aware m_t control: initialize
    M_prev = 0.1

    # Per-client control variates
    h_i = [np.zeros(d, dtype=np.float32) for _ in range(exp_cfg["N"])]

    # Training
    for t in trange(1, exp_cfg["T"]+1):
        # Cohort sampling (fixed size)
        cohort = rng.choice(exp_cfg["N"], size=n_t, replace=False)
        # Set sketch dim m_t
        m_t = choose_m_t(d=d, n_t=n_t, eps_eff_t=eps_eff_round, M_t=M_prev, kappa=1.2)
        sketch_seed = int(rng.integers(1, 2**31-1))
        sketch = CountSketch(d=d, m=m_t, seed=sketch_seed)
        # Per-node target variance scaled for sigma_eff_global; distribute equally across nodes
        node_list = list(set([node for round_idx in range(1, t+1) for node in tree_nodes_for_round(round_idx)]))
        scale_factor = (sigma_eff_global**2) / max(1e-6, np.mean(list(target_var_per_node.values())))
        target_var_scaled = {k: v*scale_factor for k, v in target_var_per_node.items()}

        # DP quantile clipping: collect per-layer norms via DP histogram
        # For simplicity, we use a single global threshold here; layer-wise requires mapping params to layers and repeating histogram per layer
        per_client_norms = []
        # Clients compute residuals and propose gating scores
        uploads = []  # each is dict with 'sketch', 'shares'
        ON_flags = []
        for i in cohort:
            idxs = client_indices[i]
            loader = DataLoader(Subset(trainset, idxs), batch_size=exp_cfg["batch_size"], shuffle=True)
            r_i = client_compute_residual(global_model, loader, device, h_i[i], c_t_vec)
            per_client_norms.append(np.linalg.norm(r_i))
        # DP histogram to set clipping threshold (P90)
        C_res_t = private_quantile_from_histogram(np.array(per_client_norms), q=0.9, clip_max=float(np.percentile(per_client_norms, 99.9)+1.0), eps=eps_clip_round, rng=rng)

        # Gate threshold schedule (public); aim for ON rate ~0.5 here; simple heuristic using previous norms
        theta_t = 0.0  # threshold on the combined score; tune schedule per phase

        # Client uploads (with clipping, sketch, DDG shares, DP gate)
        realized_ON = 0
        for i, i_client in enumerate(cohort):
            idxs = client_indices[i_client]
            loader = DataLoader(Subset(trainset, idxs), batch_size=exp_cfg["batch_size"], shuffle=True)
            r_i = client_compute_residual(global_model, loader, device, h_i[i_client], c_t_vec)
            # Clip
            norm = np.linalg.norm(r_i) + 1e-12
            scale = min(1.0, C_res_t / norm)
            r_i = (r_i * scale).astype(np.float32)
            # Score and gate
            cos_sim = float(np.dot(r_i, c_t_vec) / (np.linalg.norm(r_i)*np.linalg.norm(c_t_vec)+1e-12)) if np.linalg.norm(c_t_vec)>0 else 0.0
            s_i = max(cos_sim, 0.1 * np.linalg.norm(r_i) / max(C_res_t, 1e-6))
            ON = dp_gate(s_i, theta_t, sensitivity=1.0, eps=eps_gate_round, rng=rng)
            ON_flags.append(ON)
            realized_ON += int(ON)
            # Sketch
            y_i = sketch.S(r_i)
            # DDG-TREE shares in sketch domain
            shares_i = client_ddg_tree_shares(round_t=t, sketch_dim=m_t, seeds=vrf_seeds, target_var_per_node=target_var_scaled, L_min=L_min, cohort_size=n_t)
            if not ON:
                # Zero out sketch payload but keep DDG shares
                y_i = np.zeros_like(y_i)
            uploads.append({"y": y_i.astype(np.float32), "shares": shares_i})
            # Update client control variate h_i with server c_t
            h_i[i_client] = (1-alpha_cv)*h_i[i_client] + alpha_cv * c_t_vec

        # SecAgg sum of sketches + DDG shares (server sees only sums)
        Y_sum = np.sum([u["y"] for u in uploads], axis=0)
        Shares_sum = np.sum([u["shares"] for u in uploads], axis=0)
        sketch_agg = Y_sum + Shares_sum.astype(np.float32)
        # Unsketch to model space
        agg_update = sketch.unS(sketch_agg)

        # Server momentum + model update
        vec = flatten_params(global_model).cpu().numpy()
        vec_new = vec + agg_update / max(realized_ON, 1)  # normalization by ON clients
        assign_params(global_model, torch.tensor(vec_new, dtype=torch.float32))

        # Update c_t
        c_t_vec = (1-beta) * c_t_vec + beta * (agg_update / max(realized_ON, 1))

        # Accountant update (effective Gaussian + pure eps for gate/clip)
        accountant.add_gaussian_event(noise_multiplier=sigma_eff_global, sampling_probability=q)
        accountant.add_laplace_pure_eps(eps_gate_round)
        accountant.add_laplace_pure_eps(eps_clip_round)

        # Log to ledger
        write_ledger_record(exp_cfg["ledger_path"], {
            "round": t, "vrf_seeds": {str(k): int(v) for k, v in vrf_seeds.items()},
            "target_var_per_node": {str(k): float(v*1.0) for k, v in target_var_scaled.items()},
            "L_min": int(L_min), "realized_cohort": int(n_t),
            "sketch_seed": int(sketch_seed), "sketch_m": int(m_t),
            "clip_quantiles": {"global": float(C_res_t)},
            "gate_theta": float(theta_t),
            "eps_eff_round": float(eps_eff_round),
            "eps_gate_round": float(eps_gate_round),
            "eps_clip_round": float(eps_clip_round),
            "restart_mode": "NoTreeRestart"
        })

        # Evaluate periodically and compute SNR/residual shrinkage statistics, save to metrics log (omitted for brevity)

    final_eps = accountant.get_epsilon()
    print("Total epsilon:", final_eps)
    return global_model

Primary metrics and analysis
- Utility: test accuracy vs rounds and final accuracy at matched Оө; average across 3 seeds; report mean Вұ 95% CI.
- Residual shrinkage: E||r_i|| / E||g_i|| as a function of t (approximate ||g_i|| via initial rounds or local gradient norms without residualization).
- Clipping utilization: fraction of clients with norm > C_res,t.
- SNR per round: ||ОјМӮ_res,t|| / sqrt(Var(noise along ОјМӮ_res,t)) estimated from ledger and reconstructed noise covariance via auditor.
- Noise growth: compute RMS of reconstructed noise across rounds; fit slope of log RMS vs log T; expect вүҲ 0.5Г— slope for вҲҡlog T versus вҲҡT.

Reliability knobs
- 3 independent seeds per condition; report CI via t-intervals.
- Fixed-size cohort draws with enforced size; independent RNG streams for cohort, DDG, sketches.
- Sanity checks: compare PACER with ablations to isolate which module contributes to gains; ensure accountant Оө never exceeds target.

Experiment 2 вҖ” CommunicationвҖ“efficiency frontier at fixed DP

Objective and hypotheses
- Demonstrate 20вҖ“50% uplink reduction for PACER-FL at Оө=2, Оҙ=1e-6 with no accuracy loss by aligning sketch error to DP noise floor and privately gating low-value clients.

Data/model and setup
- EMNIST-Balanced, CNN (~1.0M params). N=5,000 clients; Оұ_dir=0.1; rounds T=500; cohort n_t=100; same local training settings as Exp.1.
- Privacy: Оө=2 at horizon, Оҙ=1e-6. Budget split as in Exp.1.

Methods
- Baseline C: DP-FedAvg + i.i.d. Gaussian + QSGD-8bit quantization.
- Baseline D: DP-FedAvg + i.i.d. Gaussian + Top-k sparsification (k=1%) with error feedback (use torch.Tensor.topk and EF from Deep Gradient Compression implementations).
- Baseline E: Private FO with Adapt Norm (independent Gaussian assumption) + its autotuned compression.
- PACER-FL full and PACER-wo-gate.

Implementation specifics
- Communication counting
  вҖў CountSketch: m_t 32-bit floats + 64-bit sketch seed id + ~256-bit SecAgg overhead.
  вҖў QSGD-8bit: 8 bits per value + per-layer scale overhead; include seed/indices if needed.
  вҖў Top-k: transmit k indices (log2 d bits each) + k values (16 bits) + EF checksum; we count the exact bits using numpy.ceil(np.log2(d)).

- FO alignment check: compute ratio of empirical sketch MSE to DP noise MSE per round; maintain in [0.7, 1.3].

Selected code snippets

# Estimated DP noise MSE vs sketch MSE

def estimate_dp_noise_mse(sigma_eff, d):
    return d * (sigma_eff**2)

def estimate_sketch_mse(x, sketch: CountSketch):
    y = sketch.S(x)
    x_hat = sketch.unS(y)
    return float(np.mean((x_hat - x)**2))

# QSGD-8bit placeholder using PyTorch quantize_per_tensor

def qsgd8_quantize(x: torch.Tensor):
    scale = x.abs().max() / 127.0 + 1e-12
    q = torch.clamp(torch.round(x / scale), -127, 127).to(torch.int8)
    return q, float(scale)

# Top-k sparsification with EF state

def topk_sparsify(x: torch.Tensor, k_frac=0.01, ef_state=None):
    d = x.numel(); k = max(1, int(d * k_frac))
    if ef_state is None: ef_state = torch.zeros_like(x)
    x_ef = x + ef_state
    vals, idx = torch.topk(x_ef.abs().flatten(), k)
    mask = torch.zeros_like(x_ef.flatten(), dtype=torch.bool)
    mask[idx] = True
    sgn = torch.sign(x_ef.flatten()[mask])
    sparse_vals = x_ef.flatten()[mask]
    # Update EF
    x_hat = torch.zeros_like(x_ef.flatten())
    x_hat[mask] = sparse_vals
    ef_state = (x_ef.flatten() - x_hat).reshape_as(x)
    return sparse_vals, idx, ef_state

Metrics
- Uplink bits per client/round and total; wall-clock proxy (bytes processed/sec) per round.
- Accuracy at horizon and AUC of accuracy vs bits curve across rounds.
- Gating: OFF rate, ON-rate fairness across strata (e.g., by dominant class per client or device tier).
- Alignment ratio: sketch MSE / DP noise MSE target in [0.7, 1.3].

Success criteria
- PACER-FL вүҘ 25% less uplink than the best baseline at similar accuracy and privacy.
- OFF/ON indistinguishability: server sees only aggregate sketches; we confirm with a classifier that attempts to infer ON-rate from aggregates performs at chance.

Reliability
- Same 3-seed protocol; ensure same accountant achieves Оө=2 for all methods; for baselines, calibrate Gaussian noise to match Оө using dp_accounting; equalize clipping thresholds.

Experiment 3 вҖ” Auditability and robustness (dropouts, L_min, restarts)

Objective and hypotheses
- Validate that client-synthesized DDG-TREE noise meets/exceeds target covariance under dropouts and varying cohorts; confirm robust accuracy under restart regimes (NoTreeRestart, SometimesRestart, TreeRestart); fairness of DP gating with min-ON constraints.

Setup
- CIFAR-10, Оө=2, Оҙ=1e-6; same as Exp.1 otherwise.
- Regimes
  вҖў R1 Mild dropouts: p=0.2; cohort n_t maintained (server resamples until size met).
  вҖў R2 Heavy dropouts: p=0.6; realized cohort may drop below L_min in ~10% rounds; engage rescue noise per ledger.
  вҖў R3 Bursty participation: alternating phases p in {0.2, 0.8}; use SometimesRestart schedule synchronized to phase boundaries.

Tree restart schedules
- NoTreeRestart: nodes persist.
- SometimesRestart: restart at known checkpoints (e.g., powers of 2 or phase boundaries) to adapt noise allocation.
- TreeRestart: fully restart tree and seeds at evenly spaced intervals.

Code stub for restart schedule

def tree_restart_schedule(T, mode="NoTreeRestart", checkpoints=None):
    if mode == "NoTreeRestart":
        return {t: tree_nodes_for_round(t) for t in range(1, T+1)}
    if mode == "SometimesRestart":
        if checkpoints is None: checkpoints = [int(T*0.33), int(T*0.66)]
        mapping = {}
        start = 1
        for cp in checkpoints + [T+1]:
            for t in range(start, cp):
                nodes = []
                k, x = 0, (t-start+1)
                while x > 0:
                    if x & 1: nodes.append((k, start + ((t-start+1) >> k) << k))
                    k += 1; x >>= 1
                mapping[t] = nodes
            start = cp
        return mapping
    if mode == "TreeRestart":
        period = max(1, int(np.sqrt(T)))
        mapping = {}
        for t in range(1, T+1):
            start_block = ((t-1)//period)*period + 1
            x = (t - start_block + 1)
            nodes = []
            k = 0
            while x > 0:
                if x & 1: nodes.append((k, start_block + ((t-start_block+1)>>k)<<k))
                k += 1; x >>= 1
            mapping[t] = nodes
        return mapping
    raise ValueError("Unknown mode")

Auditor procedures
- From ledger, reconstruct aggregate sketch-domain noise per round using auditor_reconstruct_noise; unsketch using published sketch parameters to obtain model-space noise; compute empirical covariance across repeated runs.
- Verify PSD and that covariance along each tree direction вүҘ target (within tolerance). Also confirm number of rescue-noise activations matches ledger counts and that post-processed covariance meets target in rounds with L < L_min.
- Evaluate accuracy degradation vs NoTreeRestart in R1; ensure robustness gap вүӨ 1.5 points in R2/R3.
- Fairness: enforce min-ON 0.2 with DP-throttled threshold adjustments; compute per-stratum ON rates (privately) and accuracy gaps.

Auditor core code skeleton (offline)

def auditor_verify_ledger(ledger_path):
    import json
    recs = [json.loads(l) for l in open(ledger_path)]
    ok = True
    for rec in recs:
        t = rec["round"]; m = rec["sketch_m"]
        seeds = {eval(k): v for k, v in rec["vrf_seeds"].items()}
        vars_node = {eval(k): v for k, v in rec["target_var_per_node"].items()}
        agg_noise = auditor_reconstruct_noise(t, m, seeds, vars_node, rec["L_min"], rec["realized_cohort"])
        # Compute empirical variance proxy
        var_emp = np.var(agg_noise)
        var_target_mean = np.mean(list(vars_node.values()))
        if var_emp + 1e-6 < 0.8 * var_target_mean:
            print(f"Round {t}: variance {var_emp} below target mean {var_target_mean}")
            ok = False
    return ok

Success metrics
- Audit passes: reconstructed covariances meet/exceed targets across rounds.
- Rescue noise triggers in expected fraction of rounds when realized cohort < L_min.
- Accuracy within 1.5 points across restart regimes; SometimesRestart appropriate for bursty participation.
- Fairness: worst-stratum ON rate вүҘ 0.2 with вүӨ 0.3Оө extra budget; accuracy disparity not increased.

Ablation controls (all experiments)
- w/o residualization: set Оұ=0, h_i frozen at 0; keep other modules.
- w/o DP gating: always ON; remove Laplace AboveThreshold; reallocate Оө_gate to tree noise if holding Оө fixed.
- w/o FO-aware compression: fix m to 1/8 of d; report misalignment metric.
- w/o DP quantiles: fixed clipping C_res; choose from small grid {median norm, 90th pct, 95th pct} non-DP.

Reliability and validity enhancements
- Multiple seeds and CIs; paired comparisons across seeds for higher power.
- Strict transparency ledger for public randomness seeds, sketch params, L_min, cohort sizes, and accountant budgets per round.
- Conservative accounting: approximate fixed-size sampling by Poisson q for RDP; validate with sensitivity analyses (vary q by Вұ10%).
- Audit script independent from training process; auditors need only ledger + model dimension + sketch code to verify noise guarantees.
- Sanity checks: compare aggregate DDG-TREE noise empirical distribution with discrete Gaussian (integer-rounded Gaussian) via QQ-plot; ensure no significant deviation.

Compute and runtime notes
- Simulation of 10k clients is memory-bound if naively storing per-client h_i; store h_i on disk or in memory-mapped arrays; or maintain h_i only for active clients plus a hash-based reservoir for inactive (approximation).
- Vectorize client residual computations when possible by batching synthetic clients with similar data sizes.
- Use mixed precision for local training to speed up; keep DP ops in float32.

Deliverables and plots
- Exp.1: accuracy vs rounds; final accuracy vs Оө; residual norm trajectories; clipping utilization; SNR over rounds; log RMS noise vs log T slopes.
- Exp.2: accuracy vs bits curves; total uplink reduction; alignment ratio across rounds; ON/OFF rates and fairness (strata box plots).
- Exp.3: covariance verification heatmaps; rescue-noise counts; accuracy across restart regimes; fairness metrics.
- Release ledger and auditor script to reproduce noise verification.

What differs in baselines
- Baseline A (DP-FedAvg with i.i.d. Gaussian): set Gaussian std per round using dp_accounting to hit target Оө under same q, T; no tree labels; no sketch alignment; optional QSGD/Top-k as in Exp.2.
- Baseline B (server DP-FTRL): emulate tree aggregation centrally; keep server-only noise; do not publish per-node seeds; no sketch alignment; same clipping.
- Baseline E (Adapt Norm): use its autotuned compression rule with independent Gaussian assumption; no residuals or gating; standard accountant.

Checklists per experiment
- Exp.1
  вҖў Verify budget split and accountant Оө вүӨ target.
  вҖў Record residual shrinkage and clipping utilization.
  вҖў Train with 3 seeds per Оө.
  вҖў Fit noise growth slopes.
- Exp.2
  вҖў Match Оө across methods; record bits exactly.
  вҖў Track alignment ratio per round.
  вҖў Evaluate fairness of gating.
- Exp.3
  вҖў Run R1вҖ“R3 with appropriate restart schedule.
  вҖў Run auditor verification; publish results.
  вҖў Measure rescue-noise activations.

Known limitations and mitigations
- Accounting approximation for tree-correlated noise via effective Gaussian steps may be conservative; report sensitivity across Вұ10% Пғ_eff.
- DP histogram for quantiles assumes bounded norms; we clip to a high percentile to bound sensitivity; verify chosen clip_max covers observed norms.
- SecAgg is simulated as summation; in production, integrate with an existing SecAgg library; our linear operations remain valid.

Summary of expected outcomes
- Exp.1: PACER-FL вүҘ 2вҖ“5 point accuracy gains at ОөвҲҲ[1,5] over i.i.d.-noise DP-FedAvg; clear residual shrinkage and вҲҡlog T noise growth signature.
- Exp.2: вүҘ25% uplink saving at matched accuracy and privacy; compression error tracking DP noise; ON/OFF indistinguishable under SecAgg.
- Exp.3: Public audit verifies noise вүҘ target under all dropout regimes; small accuracy deltas across restart schedules; fairness constraints effective within small extra Оө.
experimental_design.experiment_code:
# PACER-FL Experiments: Auditable DDG-TREE + Residual DP Updates + DP Gating + FO-aware Compression
# Author: Creative AI Researcher
# Notes:
# - This script implements three experiments outlined in the specification.
# - It includes robust fallbacks if optional DP libraries are not available.
# - It uses PyTorch exclusively for deep learning; NumPy for array utilities.
# - All plots are saved as .pdf files suitable for inclusion in academic papers.
# - A quick test is provided to verify functionality in under a minute on CPU by using synthetic data and small configs.
# - For full experiments, increase dataset sizes, clients, and rounds per the specification.

import os
import json
import math
import time
import random
import numpy as np
import matplotlib
matplotlib.use("Agg")  # headless
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F
from torch.utils.data import DataLoader, Dataset, Subset

# Optional deps (fall back if unavailable)
try:
    from torchvision import datasets, transforms
    _HAS_TORCHVISION = True
except Exception:
    _HAS_TORCHVISION = False

try:
    from dp_accounting import rdp as rdp_mod
    _HAS_DP_ACCOUNTING = True
except Exception:
    _HAS_DP_ACCOUNTING = False

# diffprivlib optional
try:
    from diffprivlib.mechanisms import Laplace as DPLaplace
    from diffprivlib.tools import histogram as dp_histogram
    _HAS_DIFFPRIVLIB = True
except Exception:
    _HAS_DIFFPRIVLIB = False

###############################################################################
# Utilities: RNG, printing required libraries
###############################################################################

REQUIRED_LIBRARIES = [
    "torch", "numpy", "matplotlib", "seaborn", "tqdm (optional)",
    "torchvision (recommended for CIFAR/EMNIST)",
    "dp-accounting (recommended)", "diffprivlib (recommended)", "pandas (optional)", "scipy (optional)", "pillow (for torchvision)"
]

def print_required_libraries():
    print("Required/Recommended Python libraries:")
    for lib in REQUIRED_LIBRARIES:
        print(" -", lib)


def set_seed(seed: int = 0):
    random.seed(seed)
    np.random.seed(seed)
    torch.manual_seed(seed)

###############################################################################
# Data: Synthetic dataset for quick tests + optional CIFAR10/EMNIST loaders
###############################################################################

class SyntheticImageDataset(Dataset):
    """Small synthetic dataset for quick tests.
    Creates two classes of 8x8 or 16x16 RGB images with simple patterns.
    """
    def __init__(self, n_per_class=50, img_size=8, num_classes=10, seed=0):
        rng = np.random.default_rng(seed)
        self.num_classes = num_classes
        self.images = []
        self.labels = []
        for c in range(num_classes):
            for _ in range(n_per_class):
                base = rng.random((3, img_size, img_size), dtype=np.float32)
                base += (c / max(num_classes-1, 1)) * 0.25
                base = np.clip(base, 0, 1)
                self.images.append(base.astype(np.float32))
                self.labels.append(c)
        self.images = np.stack(self.images)
        self.labels = np.array(self.labels, dtype=np.int64)
    def __len__(self):
        return len(self.labels)
    def __getitem__(self, idx):
        x = torch.tensor(self.images[idx])
        y = int(self.labels[idx])
        return x, y


def load_cifar10(data_root: str, seed: int = 0, limit_per_class: int = None):
    if not _HAS_TORCHVISION:
        raise RuntimeError("torchvision not available. Use synthetic dataset in quick test mode.")
    transform_train = transforms.Compose([
        transforms.RandomCrop(32, padding=4), transforms.RandomHorizontalFlip(),
        transforms.ToTensor(), transforms.Normalize((0.4914,0.4822,0.4465), (0.2023,0.1994,0.2010))
    ])
    transform_test = transforms.Compose([
        transforms.ToTensor(), transforms.Normalize((0.4914,0.4822,0.4465), (0.2023,0.1994,0.2010))
    ])
    trainset = datasets.CIFAR10(root=data_root, train=True, download=True, transform=transform_train)
    testset = datasets.CIFAR10(root=data_root, train=False, download=True, transform=transform_test)
    if limit_per_class is not None:
        # Reduce dataset size for faster runs
        train_indices = []
        counts = {c: 0 for c in range(10)}
        for i, y in enumerate(trainset.targets):
            if counts[y] < limit_per_class:
                train_indices.append(i)
                counts[y] += 1
        trainset = Subset(trainset, train_indices)
    return trainset, testset


###############################################################################
# Non-IID partitioning
###############################################################################

def dirichlet_noniid_split(labels: np.ndarray, num_clients: int, alpha: float = 0.1, min_size: int = 20, seed: int = 0):
    rng = np.random.default_rng(seed)
    K = int(labels.max()) + 1
    idx_by_class = [np.where(labels == k)[0] for k in range(K)]
    for idx in idx_by_class:
        rng.shuffle(idx)
    proportions = rng.dirichlet(alpha=np.ones(num_clients), size=K)  # K x C
    client_indices = [[] for _ in range(num_clients)]
    for k in range(K):
        idx = idx_by_class[k]
        # Size allocated to each client proportional to the Dirichlet draw
        sizes = np.floor(proportions[k] / proportions[k].sum() * len(idx)).astype(int)
        # Adjust to ensure total matches
        while sizes.sum() < len(idx):
            sizes[rng.integers(0, num_clients)] += 1
        while sizes.sum() > len(idx):
            j = rng.integers(0, num_clients)
            if sizes[j] > 0:
                sizes[j] -= 1
        start = 0
        for c in range(num_clients):
            end = start + sizes[c]
            client_indices[c].extend(idx[start:end].tolist())
            start = end
    for c in range(num_clients):
        if len(client_indices[c]) < min_size:
            needed = min_size - len(client_indices[c])
            pool = np.setdiff1d(np.arange(len(labels)), np.array(client_indices[c], dtype=int), assume_unique=False)
            add = rng.choice(pool, size=min(needed, len(pool)), replace=False)
            client_indices[c].extend(add.tolist())
    return [np.array(sorted(list(set(idx))), dtype=int) for idx in client_indices]


def iid_split(num_samples: int, num_clients: int, min_size: int = 20, seed: int = 0):
    rng = np.random.default_rng(seed)
    indices = np.arange(num_samples)
    rng.shuffle(indices)
    splits = np.array_split(indices, num_clients)
    res = []
    for arr in splits:
        if len(arr) < min_size:
            extra = rng.choice(indices, size=min_size - len(arr), replace=False)
            arr = np.concatenate([arr, extra])
        res.append(np.array(sorted(list(set(arr))), dtype=int))
    return res

###############################################################################
# Model definitions
###############################################################################

class SmallCIFAR10CNN(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, num_classes=10):
        super().__init__()
        self.conv1 = nn.Conv2d(3, 32, 3, padding=1)
        self.conv2 = nn.Conv2d(32, 64, 3, padding=1)
        self.pool = nn.MaxPool2d(2, 2)
        self.conv3 = nn.Conv2d(64, 128, 3, padding=1)
        self.conv4 = nn.Conv2d(128, 128, 3, padding=1)
        self.fc1 = nn.Linear(8*8*128, 256)
        self.fc2 = nn.Linear(256, num_classes)
    def forward(self, x):
        x = F.relu(self.conv1(x))
        x = F.relu(self.conv2(x))
        x = self.pool(x)
        x = F.relu(self.conv3(x))
        x = F.relu(self.conv4(x))
        x = self.pool(x)
        x = torch.flatten(x, 1)
        x = F.relu(self.fc1(x))
        x = self.fc2(x)
        return x


###############################################################################
# Flatten/unflatten helpers
###############################################################################

def flatten_params(model: nn.Module) -> torch.Tensor:
    return torch.cat([p.detach().reshape(-1) for p in model.parameters()])


def assign_params(model: nn.Module, vec: torch.Tensor):
    offset = 0
    for p in model.parameters():
        num = p.numel()
        p.data.copy_(vec[offset:offset+num].reshape(p.shape))
        offset += num


###############################################################################
# CountSketch and FO-aware dimension selection
###############################################################################

class CountSketch:
    def __init__(self, d: int, m: int, seed: int = 0):
        rng = np.random.default_rng(seed)
        self.h = rng.integers(0, m, size=d)
        self.s = rng.choice([-1, 1], size=d)
        self.m = m
        self.d = d
    def S(self, x: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
        y = np.zeros(self.m, dtype=np.float32)
        np.add.at(y, self.h, self.s * x)
        return y
    def unS(self, y: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
        counts = np.bincount(self.h, minlength=self.m).astype(np.float32)
        inv = np.where(counts > 0, 1.0 / counts, 0.0)
        bucket_vals = y * inv
        return bucket_vals[self.h] * self.s


def choose_m_t(d: int, n_t: int, eps_eff_t: float, M_t: float, kappa: float = 1.2) -> int:
    m_est = int(kappa * min(d, max(8, (n_t * eps_eff_t * max(M_t, 1e-6))**2)))
    return int(max(16, min(d, m_est)))


def bits_per_client_countsketch(m: int, value_bits: int = 32, seed_bits: int = 64, extra_overhead_bits: int = 256) -> int:
    return m * value_bits + seed_bits + extra_overhead_bits


###############################################################################
# Tree schedule and DDG-TREE noise generation (client-side) + Auditor
###############################################################################

def tree_nodes_for_round(t: int):
    nodes = []
    k, x = 0, t
    while x > 0:
        if x & 1:
            nodes.append((k, (t >> k) << k))
        k += 1
        x >>= 1
    return nodes


def ddg_int_gaussian(shape, sigma, rng: np.random.Generator):
    return np.rint(rng.normal(0.0, sigma, size=shape)).astype(np.int64)


def client_ddg_tree_shares(round_t: int, sketch_dim: int, seeds: dict, target_var_per_node: dict, L_min: int, cohort_size: int):
    rngs = {k: np.random.default_rng(seeds[k]) for k in seeds}
    shares = np.zeros(sketch_dim, dtype=np.int64)
    for node in tree_nodes_for_round(round_t):
        sigma = math.sqrt(target_var_per_node[node])
        scale = math.sqrt(max(L_min, cohort_size))
        z = ddg_int_gaussian(sketch_dim, sigma/scale, rngs[node])
        shares += z
    return shares


def auditor_reconstruct_noise(round_t: int, sketch_dim: int, seeds: dict, target_var_per_node: dict, L_min: int, realized_L: int):
    rngs = {k: np.random.default_rng(seeds[k]) for k in seeds}
    agg = np.zeros(sketch_dim, dtype=np.int64)
    for node in tree_nodes_for_round(round_t):
        sigma = math.sqrt(target_var_per_node[node])
        z = ddg_int_gaussian(sketch_dim, sigma, rngs[node])
        agg += z
    return agg


def tree_restart_schedule(T: int, mode: str = "NoTreeRestart", checkpoints=None):
    if mode == "NoTreeRestart":
        return {t: tree_nodes_for_round(t) for t in range(1, T+1)}
    if mode == "SometimesRestart":
        if checkpoints is None:
            checkpoints = [int(T*0.33), int(T*0.66)]
        mapping = {}
        start = 1
        for cp in checkpoints + [T+1]:
            for t in range(start, cp):
                nodes = []
                k, x = 0, (t-start+1)
                while x > 0:
                    if x & 1:
                        nodes.append((k, start + ((t-start+1) >> k) << k))
                    k += 1
                    x >>= 1
                mapping[t] = nodes
            start = cp
        return mapping
    if mode == "TreeRestart":
        period = max(1, int(math.sqrt(T)))
        mapping = {}
        for t in range(1, T+1):
            start_block = ((t-1)//period)*period + 1
            x = (t - start_block + 1)
            nodes = []
            k = 0
            while x > 0:
                if x & 1:
                    nodes.append((k, start_block + ((t-start_block+1)>>k)<<k))
                k += 1
                x >>= 1
            mapping[t] = nodes
        return mapping
    raise ValueError("Unknown mode")


###############################################################################
# DP mechanisms: DP gate (AboveThreshold) and DP quantile via DP histogram
###############################################################################

def laplace_noise(scale: float, rng: np.random.Generator = None) -> float:
    if rng is None:
        rng = np.random.default_rng()
    u = rng.random() - 0.5
    return -scale * math.copysign(1.0, u) * math.log(1 - 2*abs(u) + 1e-12)


def dp_gate(score: float, theta: float, sensitivity: float, eps: float, rng: np.random.Generator = None) -> bool:
    if _HAS_DIFFPRIVLIB:
        mech = DPLaplace(epsilon=max(eps, 1e-8), sensitivity=max(sensitivity, 1e-12), random_state=rng)
        noisy_score = mech.randomise(score)
        return noisy_score >= theta
    else:
        b = sensitivity / max(eps, 1e-8)
        noisy_score = score + laplace_noise(b, rng=rng)
        return noisy_score >= theta


def private_quantile_from_histogram(norms: np.ndarray, q: float = 0.9, clip_max: float = 10.0, eps: float = 0.05, bins: int = 50, rng: np.random.Generator = None) -> float:
    if rng is None:
        rng = np.random.default_rng()
    norms = np.clip(norms, 0.0, clip_max)
    if _HAS_DIFFPRIVLIB:
        counts, edges = dp_histogram(norms, range=(0.0, clip_max), bins=bins, epsilon=max(eps, 1e-8), random_state=rng)
    else:
        counts, edges = np.histogram(norms, range=(0.0, clip_max), bins=bins)
        # Add Laplace noise to each bin count with sensitivity=1
        b = 1.0 / max(eps, 1e-8)
